title: भगवद्गीता अध्याय १३ - क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञविभागयोग
titleEn: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13 - Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga
devanagariText:
॥ अथ त्रयोदशोऽध्यायः - क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञविभागयोगः ॥

अर्जुन उवाच —
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च।
एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव॥१॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच —
इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते।
एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः॥२॥

क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत।
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम॥३॥

तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक्च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत्।
स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे शृणु॥४॥

ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक्।
ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितैः॥५॥

महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च।
इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः॥६॥

इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतना धृतिः।
एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम्॥७॥

अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम्।
आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः॥८॥

इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च।
जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम्॥९॥

असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्गः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु।
नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु॥१०॥

मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी।
विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि॥११॥

अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम्।
एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा॥१२॥

ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्नुते।
अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते॥१३॥

सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम्।
सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति॥१४॥

सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम्।
असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च॥१५॥

बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च।
सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत्॥१६॥

अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम्।
भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च॥१७॥

ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते।
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम्॥१८॥

इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः।
मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते॥१९॥

प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि।
विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान्॥२०॥

पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान्।
कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु॥२२॥

उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः।
परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः॥२३॥

य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैः सह।
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते॥२४॥

क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा।
भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम्॥३५॥

॥ इति श्रीमद्भगवद्गीतासूपनिषत्सु ब्रह्मविद्यायां योगशास्त्रे
श्रीकृष्णार्जुनसंवादे क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञविभागयोगो नाम त्रयोदशोऽध्यायः ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Trayodasho'dhyayah - Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yogah ||

Arjuna Uvacha —
Prakritim Purusham Chaiva Kshetram Kshetrajnam-Eva Cha.
Etad-Veditum-Ichchhami Jnanam Jneyam Cha Keshava.||1||

Shri Bhagavan Uvacha —
Idam Shariram Kaunteya Kshetram-Ity-Abhidhiyate.
Etad-Yo Vetti Tam Prahuh Kshetrajna Iti Tad-Vidah.||2||

Kshetrajnam Cha-Api Mam Viddhi Sarva-Kshetreshu Bharata.
Kshetra-Kshetrajnayor-Jnanam Yat-Taj-Jnanam Matam Mama.||3||

Amanitvam-Adambhitvam-Ahimsa Kshantir-Arjavam.
Acharyo-Upasanam Shaucham Sthairyam-Atma-Vinigrahah.||8||

Indriya-Artheshu Vairagyam-Anahankara Eva Cha.
Janma-Mrityu-Jara-Vyadhi-Duhkha-Dosha-Anudarshanam.||9||

Mayi Cha-Ananya-Yogena Bhaktir-Avyabhicharini.
Vivikta-Desha-Sevitvam-Aratir-Jana-Samsadi.||11||

Adhyatma-Jnana-Nityatvam Tattva-Jnana-Artha-Darshanam.
Etaj-Jnanam-Iti Proktam-Ajnanam Yad-Ato'nyatha.||12||

Jneyam Yat-Tat Pravakshyami Yaj-Jnatva-Amritam-Ashnute.
Anadi-Mat-Param Brahma Na Sat-Tan-Na-Asad-Uchyate.||13||

Sarvatah Pani-Padam Tat Sarvato'kshi-Shiro-Mukham.
Sarvatah Shrutimat-Loke Sarvam-Avritya Tishthati.||14||

Jyotisham-Api Taj-Jyotis-Tamasah Param-Uchyate.
Jnanam Jneyam Jnana-Gamyam Hridi Sarvasya Vishthitam.||18||

Upadrashta-Anumanta Cha Bharta Bhokta Maheshvarah.
Parama-Atme-Ti Cha-Apy-Ukto Dehe'smin Purushah Parah.||23||

Kshetra-Kshetrajnayor-Evam-Antaram Jnana-Chakshsusha.
Bhuta-Prakriti-Moksham Cha Ye Vidur-Yanti Te Param.||35||

|| Iti Shrimad-Bhagavad-Gitasu-Upanishatsu Brahma-Vidyayam Yoga-Shastre
Shri-Krishna-Arjuna-Samvade Kshetra-Kshetrajna-Vibhaga-Yogo Nama Trayodasho'dhyayah ||
hindiMeaning: त्रयोदश अध्याय 'क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञविभागयोग' में शरीर (क्षेत्र) और आत्मा (क्षेत्रज्ञ) का विवेक बताया गया है। कृष्ण कहते हैं — यह शरीर क्षेत्र है और जो इसे जानता है वह क्षेत्रज्ञ है। सब क्षेत्रों (शरीरों) में मैं ही क्षेत्रज्ञ हूँ। क्षेत्र-क्षेत्रज्ञ का ज्ञान ही सच्चा ज्ञान है। फिर बीस गुण बताए जो ज्ञान के साधन हैं — अमानित्व, अदम्भित्व, अहिंसा, क्षमा, सरलता, गुरुसेवा, शौच, स्थिरता, आत्मसंयम, वैराग्य आदि। ज्ञेय (जानने योग्य) परब्रह्म का वर्णन है — जिसके सब ओर हाथ-पाँव हैं, सब ओर नेत्र-सिर-मुख हैं, जो ज्योतियों की भी ज्योति है, अन्धकार से परे है। प्रकृति और पुरुष — दोनों अनादि हैं। जो ज्ञान-चक्षु से क्षेत्र और क्षेत्रज्ञ का अंतर जानता है, वह मोक्ष प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Teaches the distinction between body (matter) and soul (consciousness)
Lists twenty qualities that constitute true spiritual knowledge
Describes the Supreme Brahman as the light of all lights
Explains the relationship between Prakriti and Purusha
Leads to liberation through discriminative knowledge
Develops virtues like humility, non-violence, and forgiveness
description: The Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13 - Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga, known in Sanskrit as भगवद्गीता अध्याय १३ - क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञविभागयोग, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Krishna. Rooted in Bhagavad Gita - Mahabharata, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to describe the Supreme Brahman as the light of all lights and teach the distinction between body (matter) and soul (consciousness). It is also said to develop virtues like humility, non-violence, and forgiveness. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami. Comprising 35 verses, it can be completed in approximately 16 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13 - Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13 — Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13 (भगवद्गीता अध्याय १३), titled Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (the Yoga of the Distinction between the Field and the Knower of the Field), is the thirteenth chapter of the Srimad Bhagavad Gita and the opening chapter of the final hexad (Chapters 13-18) which expounds the supreme jnana. It contains 35 verses (some recensions count 34, depending on whether Arjuna's opening question is counted). The chapter teaches the central Vedantic distinction between kshetra (the field — the body and all its components) and kshetrajna (the knower of the field — the indwelling Self/Atman that witnesses the body), and identifies the Supreme Self as the kshetrajna in all kshetras. It then lists the twenty qualities that constitute jnana and describes Brahman, prakriti, purusha and the supreme Purushottama. (Source: Srimad Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13; Mahabharata Bhishma Parva.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of kshetra and kshetrajna in Chapter 13?
seoFaqs.answer: Krishna defines them in verse 13.1-13.2: idam shariram Kaunteya kshetram-ity-abhidhiyate, etad yo vetti tam prahuh kshetrajna iti tad-vidah — This body, O Kaunteya, is called the kshetra (the field); the one who knows this is called the kshetrajna (the knower of the field) by the wise. The kshetra is the entire psycho-physical complex — the five gross elements, ahamkara (ego), buddhi (intellect), avyakta (unmanifest prakriti), the ten senses + mind, the five sense-objects, plus the modifications of desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, body-aggregate, consciousness and steadiness. The kshetrajna is the pure witness-consciousness (chetana) that illumines this entire field without itself being modified by it. Krishna then declares (13.3): kshetrajnam chapi mam viddhi sarva-kshetreshu Bharata — Know me also as the kshetrajna in all kshetras — the one Self present in every body. This is the foundational Vedantic teaching of the Atman-Brahman identity. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 13.1-13.3.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the twenty qualities of Jnana listed in Chapter 13?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 13.8-13.12 list the twenty virtues that are jnana (knowledge) — and the absence of which is ajnana (ignorance): (1) amanitvam — humility/absence of self-conceit, (2) adambhitvam — absence of hypocrisy, (3) ahimsa — non-violence, (4) kshanti — forbearance, (5) arjava — uprightness/straightforwardness, (6) acharya-upasanam — service to the teacher, (7) shaucha — purity (inner and outer), (8) sthairya — steadiness, (9) atma-vinigraha — self-restraint, (10) indriya-artheshu vairagya — dispassion towards sense-objects, (11) anahamkara — absence of egoism, (12) janma-mrityu-jara-vyadhi-duhkha-doshanudarshanam — perception of the suffering and evil in birth, death, old age and disease, (13) asakti — non-attachment, (14) anabhishvanga — absence of clinging to son, wife, home etc., (15) nityam sama-chittatvam ishtanishtopapattishu — constant equanimity in the desirable and undesirable, (16) mayi cha-ananya-yogena bhakti-r-avyabhicharini — unswerving exclusive devotion to me, (17) vivikta-desha-sevitvam — resort to solitary places, (18) aratir-jana-samsadi — distaste for crowds, (19) adhyatma-jnana-nityatvam — constancy in self-knowledge, (20) tattva-jnana-artha-darshanam — perception of the goal of true knowledge. Krishna concludes — etad jnanam-iti proktam, ajnanam yad-ato'nyatha — this is jnana; whatever is opposite to this is ajnana. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 13.8-13.12.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous description of Brahman in Chapter 13 verse 14?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 13.14 (in many editions) gives one of the most poetic descriptions of Brahman in all of Hindu scripture: Sarvatah pani-padam tat sarvato'kshi-shiro-mukham, sarvatah shrutimal-loke sarvam-avritya tishthati — Everywhere are its hands and feet, everywhere its eyes, heads and faces, everywhere its ears; pervading all, it stands. Verse 13.15 continues: Sarvendriya-guna-abhasam sarvendriya-vivarjitam — apparently endowed with all the senses but actually devoid of any; unattached yet supporting all; without qualities yet experiencing all qualities. Verse 13.16: Bahir-antash-cha bhutanam acharam charameva cha — outside and inside all beings, moving and unmoving, subtle and incomprehensible, far away and near. Verse 13.17: avibhaktam cha bhuteshu vibhaktam-iva cha sthitam — undivided yet apparently divided in beings; the supporter, devourer and creator. Verse 13.18: jyotisham-api taj-jyotis tamasah param-uchyate — the light of all lights, beyond darkness; jnanam, jneyam, jnana-gamyam — knowledge, the knowable and the goal of knowledge, seated in the hearts of all. These verses are recited as a complete Brahma-stuti by Vedantins. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 13.14-13.18.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Prakriti, Purusha and Purushottama in Chapter 13?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 13.19-13.34 develop the Sankhya-Vedanta distinction between three principles: (1) Prakriti — the eternal unmanifest material cause of the body and all its modifications, the source of the three gunas (sattva, rajas, tamas), karya (effect) and karana (instrument) and the producer of pleasure and pain. (2) Purusha — the eternal individual conscious self that gets seated in prakriti and experiences the gunas; it is the cause of bondage to good and evil births. (3) Purushottama / Paramatman / Maheshvara — the Supreme Self who pervades all, transcends prakriti and purusha, is the witness, supporter, enjoyer and Lord. Krishna teaches that whoever knows this distinction — and especially knows the Self as different from prakriti and identical with the supreme — is liberated, no matter what stage of life they are in. The chapter closes (13.35) with the simile of the sun illumining the whole world, comparing it to how the kshetri (Self) illumines the entire kshetra. (Source: Bhagavad Gita 13.19-13.34.)

seoFaqs.question: When is Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13 traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Chapter 13 is recited on Day 13 of an 18-day Gita Parayana cycle. It is especially recited by jnana-marga sadhakas (followers of the path of knowledge) — Advaitins, Vishishtadvaitins and Dvaitins all draw heavily upon this chapter for their core philosophical teaching. Specific occasions include: Wednesday (Budhvar — Krishna's day), Ekadashi, Gita Jayanti (Margashirsha Shukla Ekadashi), Guru Purnima (the chapter exalts acharya-upasanam — service to the guru), and during Adi Shankara Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Panchami). The chapter is also chanted as therapy for the bereaved — its central teaching that the kshetrajna (Self) is distinct from the kshetra (body) brings comfort by reminding that what dies is only the body-field, not the witness-consciousness. The twenty qualities of jnana (verses 8-12) form a complete daily ethical code. (Sources: Gita Mahatmya; Advaita-Vedanta sampradaya niyamas.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Chapter 13 connect to the verse-by-verse Gita pages on this site?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhagavad Gita Chapter 13 — Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga contains 35 verses, each studied verse-by-verse on this site at /gita/chapter-13/verse-1 through /gita/chapter-13/verse-35. The verse-level pages provide the Devanagari, transliteration, word-by-word meaning, English translation, Hindi anvaya and commentary by classical acharyas (Shankara, Ramanuja, Madhva, Gyaneshwar) for each shloka — particularly important for the deeply philosophical Brahma-stuti verses (13.14-13.18) and the Sankhya verses (13.19-13.34) where each phrase carries multiple Vedantic implications. This stotra page presents the entire chapter as a single text for parayana — recommended for jnana-yogis and those seeking the central kshetra-kshetrajna distinction. Together with Chapters 14 and 15, it forms the philosophical heart of the Gita's final hexad. (Source: Stotra and Gita parayana traditions.)
